scenario: Xander Logistics filters 7000 records per day in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL output mapping on a service task must produce the sublist of lineItems whose priceEur is at least 100, given a process variable lineItems that is a list of contexts with a priceEur field.
question: Which FEEL expression filters the list of contexts in Camunda 8?

options.text: lineItems[item.priceEur >= 100] uses the FEEL list filter syntax with item referring to the current element of the list
filter(lineItems, item.priceEur >= 100) uses a built-in higher-order FEEL function on the list with a lambda predicate
lineItems.where(priceEur >= 100) uses the FEEL dot-where method on every list in Camunda 8 FEEL with full predicate access
lineItems.filter(priceEur >= 100) uses the FEEL dot-filter method on every list in Camunda 8 FEEL with full predicate access

a.text: Correct. FEEL list filter syntax is list[predicate]; the variable item refers to the current element when iterating contexts.

b.text: Incorrect. Camunda FEEL does not include a top-level filter() higher-order function; bracket-predicate is the documented form.

c.text: Incorrect. FEEL is not a method-chained language; .where is not a documented operator on lists.

d.text: Incorrect. Same as above; .filter is not a documented FEEL operator on lists in Camunda 8.

explanation: FEEL filters lists with bracket-predicate syntax (list[predicate]); inside the predicate, item refers to the current element.
